subject: [PATCH v5 4/9] mm/memory_hotplug: add __add_memory_driver_managed() with online_type arg

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch adds a new interface to the Linux kernel's memory hotplug functionality, allowing drivers to select their preferred online type (ZONE_NORMAL or ZONE_MOVABLE) when adding memory. The CXL driver is the primary motivator for this change, as it needs to plumb a preferred policy through the dax kmem driver. The patch refactors existing APIs and adds new helpers to support this feature, while ensuring that only known in-tree modules can override the system-wide onlining policy.
